A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.
programme.title: Zorba the Greek

programme.desc: A modern-day blacksmith becomes the toast of King Arthur's court after being transported to sixth-century England.
programme.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court

programme.desc: A bounty hunter (Glenn Ford) faces his son's killers with help from the son (Michael Burns) of an outlaw he has killed.
programme.title: Santee

programme.desc: A clumsy young man nurtures a plant and discovers that it's carnivorous, forcing him to kill to feed it.
programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors

programme.desc: John Adams (William Daniels), Benjamin Franklin (Howard da Silva) and Thomas Jefferson (Ken Howard) lead the way to the Colonies' Declaration of Independence.
Three retired Texas Rangers rescue a former colleague and eventually become the sheriff and deputies of Waco.
programme.title: The Over-the-Hill Gang Rides Again

programme.desc: A reporter writes a fictitious column about a man named John Doe, who claims to despair at America's neglect of the little people and plans to kill himself. To maintain the ruse, the newspaper hires a ballplayer-turned-hobo to pose as John Doe.
programme.title: Meet John Doe

programme.desc: A teenager (Tuesday Weld) wants to wear a strapless gown to the prom.
programme.title: Rock, Rock, Rock!

programme.desc: A stewardess (Doris Day) runs for her life once she realizes her second husband (Louis Jourdan) killed her first.
programme.title: Julie

programme.desc: Little Alice (Fiona Fullerton) falls into a strange land and meets the White Rabbit (Michael Crawford), March Hare (Peter Sellers) and other odd characters out of Lewis Carroll's story.
programme.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

programme.desc: Dorothy tries to save her dog from a storm and eases on down the road to see the Wiz with Tin Man, Scarecrow and Cowardly Lion.
programme.title: The Wiz

programme.desc: A lawyer (Ginger Rogers) bails out and then marries a Hollywood tough guy (Jack Carson) who's a milquetoast in real life.
programme.title: The Groom Wore Spurs

programme.desc: An armchair explorer (Bob Hope), a G-woman (Edie Adams), and a Soviet spy (Anita Ekberg) and her colleague go on safari for a lost moon capsule.
programme.title: Call Me Bwana

programme.desc: A framed sheriff (John McIntire) is about to hang, but his bogus wayward daughters have a plan.
programme.title: The New Daughters of Joshua Cabe

programme.desc: Jelly from Venus turns into the Ymir, a tailed creature that keeps growing and ends up on the loose in Rome.
programme.title: 20 Million Miles to Earth

programme.desc: The history of 19th-century Western expansion, as seen through the lives of three generations of a pioneer family.
programme.title: How the West Was Won

programme.desc: A California girl called Gidget (Sandra Dee) spends summer with surf bums called Moondoggie (James Darren) and Kahuna (Cliff Robertson).
programme.title: Gidget

programme.desc: Robbo (Frank Sinatra), Little John (Dean Martin), Will (Sammy Davis Jr.) and company are charitable gangsters fighting for turf in 1928 Chicago.
programme.title: Robin and the Seven Hoods

programme.desc: A Los Angeles policeman (Van Heflin) has an affair and becomes obsessed with an all-night disc jockey's wife (Evelyn Keyes).
programme.title: The Prowler

programme.desc: A bachelor (Jerry Lewis) agrees to baby-sit for a Hollywood star (Marilyn Maxwell), who turns out to have triplets.
programme.title: Rock-a-Bye Baby

programme.desc: The first manned flight to Mars is a flop, so a space official (Hal Holbrook) fakes it Hollywood-style for the public.
programme.title: Capricorn One

programme.desc: An avenger's (Richard Harrison) quest in the name of his father leads from Mexico to a duel in Texas.
programme.title: Gunfight at Red Sands

programme.desc: Mickey Spillane's private eye Mike Hammer follows bad guys and blondes to a smoking box.
programme.title: Kiss Me Deadly

programme.desc: A blind private eye (Edward Arnold) with a guide dog probes a murder for a friend (Ann Harding) and finds a Nazi spy ring.
programme.title: Eyes in the Night

programme.desc: In a society where life ends at age 30, a law enforcement officer known as a Sandman (Michael York) flees with another 30-year-old (Jenny Agutter) when he learns his number has come up prematurely.
programme.title: Logan's Run

programme.desc: A St. Louis lawyer's (Leon Ames) family (Judy Garland, Margaret O'Brien) stays in town for the 1904 World's Fair.
programme.title: Meet Me in St. Louis

programme.desc: U.S. soldiers (Van Johnson, John Hodiak, Ricardo Montalban) from all over fight in the Battle of the Bulge.
programme.title: Battleground

programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dim-witted porter that Dracula wants his brain for a monster's body.
programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein

programme.desc: A small-town Mississippi boss tries to pair his daughter (Joanne Woodward) with an enigmatic drifter (Paul Newman).
programme.title: The Long, Hot Summer

programme.desc: A secret agent blocks a villain's helio-beam plot in the second of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: Murderers' Row

programme.desc: A doctor's unconventional theories of medicine and relationship with a young student place his reputation in jeopardy.
programme.title: People Will Talk

programme.desc: A brother (Fred Astaire) and sister (Jane Powell) take their act to 1947 London. Includes Astaire's famous dance on the ceiling.
programme.title: Royal Wedding

programme.desc: A doctor (Spencer Tracy) marries a model (Hedy Lamarr) and quits his clinic to make enough money to please her.
programme.title: I Take This Woman

programme.desc: A dried-out heroin addict (Frank Sinatra) returns to his wife (Eleanor Parker) and card dealing in Chicago.
programme.title: The Man With the Golden Arm

programme.desc: The new girlfriend in the architect's life finds foul play behind his bride's death and his own torment.
programme.title: The Second Woman

programme.desc: A carefree singing priest (Bing Crosby) clashes with a no-nonsense nun (Ingrid Bergman) at a school in a struggling parish.
programme.title: The Bells of St. Mary's

programme.desc: The jazz trombonist (James Stewart) marries his sweetheart (June Allyson), forms a band and creates his signature sound.
programme.title: The Glenn Miller Story

programme.desc: A goofy astronaut (Don Knotts) with a fear of heights becomes assistant janitor, then goes on a space mission.
programme.title: The Reluctant Astronaut

programme.desc: Two broke jazzmen (Bing Crosby, Bob Hope) hop a southbound ship and save a hypnotized heiress (Dorothy Lamour) from her sinister aunt.
programme.title: Road to Rio

programme.desc: A retired cat burglar (Cary Grant) sees fireworks with an American heiress (Grace Kelly) on the Riviera.
programme.title: To Catch a Thief

programme.desc: After returning to Hawaii from being in the army, a pineapple tycoon's rebellious son turns into an island tour guide with his girlfriend.
programme.title: Blue Hawaii

programme.desc: Rebel trucker "Rubber Duck" (Kris Kristofferson) blocks a crooked sheriff (Ernest Borgnine) with a CB-linked army of big rigs.
programme.title: Convoy

programme.desc: The pirate (Robert Newton) and young Jim Hawkins (Kit Taylor) rescue Purity Pinker (Connie Gilchrist) and look for treasure.
programme.title: Long John Silver

programme.desc: Willie (Anne Baxter), a German woman, joins an American carnival passing through Munich. While traveling town to town, she joins Frank in his high-dive act, but tragedy is only a slip away.
programme.title: Carnival Story

S03 E08 — Episode 8 Saddled with a new responsibility, Jihad sees a chance to change his family's circumstances; Abu Mohammed faces fallout from the hostage fiasco.
programme.title: Fauda

programme.desc: S02 E01 — First Grade Jamal's death and the release of the terrorist Abdullah Bin-Rashid undermine the position of Yusuf; Uri and Nimrod try to push the Ministry of Defense into figuring out where Amiel is by exposing the CD they got from Ruth Shorer.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S02 E210 — Drag Milton Berle did it; so did Julie Andrews; and RuPaul works it; join Host Sara Greenfield and Gregory Cohen as they discuss the best drag on screen, from Some Like It Hot, Victor/Victoria, Mrs. Doubtfire, To Wong Foo, Thanks For Everything, and more.

programme.desc: Daily news from the State of Israel.
programme.title: ILTV News

programme.desc: S01 E204 — King David's Rise Academy Award winner Jon Voight and his companions hold one of their Bus Discussions on the way to their next location. Here, they conduct a round table retelling of the Ascension of David, each proffering their moving wisdom on the story.
programme.title: Land of Israel: God's Story

programme.desc: The Birth of Samson Samson was born during a tense time in Israel's history, when the nation was at war with its enemy, the Philistines. Samson's parents had been having a hard time conceiving. We meet the angel who bore good news, and learn G-d blesses Samson.
programme.title: Land of Israel: God's Story

programme.desc: The life of racer Rod Hall, a pioneer of the first 1000-mile desert race down the Baja peninsula. At 80 years old, Hall performed his last Baja 1000, ending a 50-year racing career.
programme.title: One More Win

programme.desc: Charity & Kindness Hanan Harchol explores Jewish thought on tzedakah (charity) and chesed (kindness) in engaging, thought-provoking conversations with his Israeli parents.
programme.title: Jewish Food For Thought

programme.desc: Adaeze connects with her Nigerian heritage, and Lenny experiments with sweet cream and kola nut for a new customer's surprise engagement party.
